fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong margins and analyst conviction, though trading at a high valuation and with significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 4.6%; next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY and analyst estimates of 236.6% for the current quarter and 300.7% for the current fiscal year indicate substantial expansion.
Operating margin of 21.8% and profit margin of 17.7% demonstrate strong profitability.
Return on equity at 22.8% is robust.
Mean analyst target of $1104.68 suggests significant upside potential from the current price, with a consensus 'buy' rating from 24 analysts.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 167.76 and Price/Sales of 29.84 indicate a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44 suggests significant leverage.
Free cash flow of $93.35M is low relative to the company's market capitalization, suggesting potential cash flow conversion issues.
Quick ratio of 0.94 is below 1, indicating potential short-term liquidity constraints.

news.paragraph: Lumentum Holdings (LITE) is being highlighted for its expanding AI connectivity portfolio, with questions arising about the durability of its growth. The company was also featured in Zacks.com's highlights, alongside news of new ETFs that include a "2X LITE" product. Separately, a foundation announced an artist residency program involving "Lite Brite Neon Studio," which is unrelated to the tech company.
